t_ch: 也许这本爱情小说里，还藏着塔米拉小姐本人对爱情的看法呢……
t_en: Maybe hidden within this novel are Tamila's secret thoughts about love...

t_ch: 谢谢你帮我这个忙！收下这些吧，这是小女子的一片心意~
t_en: Thanks lots for helping me with this! Take this as a token of my appreciation~

t_ch: 最近还顺利吗…
t_en: Everything going well?

t_ch: 托你的福，越来越顺利了~小女子越来越能理解塔米拉小姐的灵魂了。
t_en: Thanks to you, things are getting better and better~ I'm getting a clearer picture of Tamila's soul.

t_ch: 那本小说…《歌德宾馆的夜与雾》，老实说，小女子现在还不是很能读透……
t_en: That novel Evening Ember at the Goethe Hotel... to be honest, I'm not sure I really get it completely...

t_ch: 但小女子会让自己喜欢上这本小说的~
t_en: But I'll make sure that I fall in love with this novel~

t_ch: 小女子最近在想，塔米拉小姐对菲丝小姐说的那句话…「在温暖的卧室中伴着花香反复阅读这本书，真是太幸福了。」
t_en: I've been thinking about what Tamila said that time to Fizz back at the store... "It's such a blessing to read this book over and over again in a cozy warm bedroom with my flowers."

t_ch: 也许她温暖的卧室才是问题的关键~
t_en: Maybe a warm bedroom is the crucial piece of the puzzle here~

t_ch: 前几天小女子在后台听到塔米拉小姐抱怨说，家里的加热器坏掉了…既然加热器坏了，那她一定会去机械屋修理。
t_en: A few days back, I heard Tamila complaining backstage that her heater at home is broken. That being the case, she'll definitely go to the workshop to get it fixed.

t_ch: 拜托你，去机械屋问问看，塔米拉小姐平常将加热器开到多大的档位呢？
t_en: Please, can you go and ask at the workshop what level Tamila turns her heater up to?

t_ch: 这个问题…好像有点太私人了。
t_en: That's... starting to get a little too personal.

t_ch: 希露瓦会回答这种问题吗…
t_en: Will Serval even answer this kind of question?

t_ch: 就去帮我问问嘛~问不出来也没关系，小女子不会怪你。拜托啦~
t_en: Just go and ask for me~ If you can't, it's okay and I won't blame you. But please~

t_ch: 关于塔米拉…
t_en: About Tamila...

t_ch: 塔米拉？哦…有点印象，就是那位出名的歌剧演员吧。
t_en: Tamila? Ah... I remember, you mean that famous musical actress?

t_ch: 她使用加热器的习惯？问这个做什么？
t_en: How does she use her heater? What are you asking about that for?

t_ch: 哎，你这么一说，我倒是记起来了——前几天她的加热器确实坏了，吭哧吭哧地拖着个坏加热器来我这里维修。
t_en: Ah, it's coming back to me now that you mention it. Her heater broke a few days back, and she dragged it here for me to take a look at it.

t_ch: 当时我还纳闷呢，像她这种咖位的明星，身边的仆人、助理肯定少不了吧？碰到这种体力活，她居然亲自上阵…啧，真不一般。
t_en: At the time, I was still wondering what a star like her was doing without any servants or assistants around to help her, right? But seeing someone like her performing her own labor like this... well, it's uncommon, to say the least.

t_ch: 不过她的使用习惯…对不起啦，这可是用户的隐私。就算是对{NICKNAME}也不能说哦~
t_en: But I'm sorry, as for her heater usage and habits, that's personal info. Even if you were the one asking, {NICKNAME}...

t_ch: 抱歉，让你为难了。
t_en: Sorry for putting you in a difficult position.

t_ch: 没事没事，客气什么！我猜，肯定是哪个狂热粉丝自己不好意思，才托你来问的吧？理解理解。
t_en: That's cool. No worries! I'm guessing there's a fanatical fan behind the scenes who's too embarrassed to ask themselves, am I right? I can understand that.

t_ch: 嘿，这种事我可见多了。下次再碰到那种死缠烂打的粉丝，记得拔腿就跑！
t_en: Hey, I see this thing all the time. Run for the hills next time you bump into that kind of stalker!

t_ch: 转达希露瓦的话…
t_en: Relay Serval's reply...

t_ch: 没问出来？不，这信息量可比小女子想象中大多了~谢谢你~
t_en: You couldn't get the info? No, this is far more intel than I ever could have imagined. Thank you so much~

t_ch: 按照希露瓦小姐的说法，塔米拉可是亲自拖着加热器去机械屋的~不是仆人、助理或经纪人，而是亲自拖着去的……
t_en: Based on Serval's story, Tamila dragged the heater to the workshop all by herself~ No servants, assistants, or a manager helping her out — just her dragging it herself...

t_ch: 原来这才是塔米拉小姐的「灵魂」…小女子逐渐理解了一切……
t_en: So that's what Tamila's soul is like... I'm gradually coming to comprehensively understand everything about her...

t_ch: 我没太懂…
t_en: I don't quite get it...

t_ch: 塔米拉小姐的灵魂是指？
t_en: What do you mean by Tamila's soul?

t_ch: 啊，真对不起~小女子又开始自说自话了，希望没有让你困惑~
t_en: Ah, sorry~ I was talking to myself again. I didn't mean to confuse you~

t_ch: 小女子只是觉得，自己以前对塔米拉小姐的理解太浅薄了。小女子所欠缺的那种，对她「灵魂」的临摹，就藏在她舞台下的日常生活中。
t_en: I just think that this whole time, I've just been really shallow about what I've learned from Tamila. What I really need to mimic from her soul is hidden in her everyday life offstage.

t_ch: 并非是舞台上的角色和演出塑造了她独一无二的气场，而是舞台下，她对生活的热爱，将她的灵魂变成了一颗璀璨的宝石~
t_en: It's not her character and performance onstage that shape her unique soul, but rather it's her offstage everyday love for life that makes her so precious and dazzling~

t_ch: 谢谢你，从今天开始，小女子终于可以成为另一个塔米拉了。这让小女子想起了一段很美的唱词~
t_en: Thank you. Starting today, I can finally become the second Tamila. This reminds me of the lyrics of a beautiful song~

t_ch: 「夫人~夫人~您身后的阴影不知所踪~而我看着您~犹在镜中~」
t_en: "Madam~ madam~ that shadow behind you has gone~ but I see your reflection~ still in the mirror~"

t_ch: 最近还顺利吗…
t_en: Everything going well?

t_ch: 多亏了你~还挺顺利的~小女子已经能看到自己在舞台上光彩夺目的身影了…总有一天，小女子也能像她那样……
t_en: Thanks to you~ everything's going great. I can already see my dazzling figure on stage... One day, I'm going to be just like her...

t_ch: 说到这个，你能帮小女子一个小忙么？
t_en: Speaking of which — can you please help me out with just one little thing?

t_ch: 说说看吧。
t_en: Go on.

t_ch: 还有别的事…
t_en: There's more?

t_ch: 你为小女子带来的情报虽然都很重要，但毕竟还是二手信息…等传到小女子耳朵里，那都成了三手信息啦~
t_en: All this intel you've brought me is fantastic, but it's still second-hand info... and by the time it reaches me, it's already third-hand...

t_ch: 能不能请你亲眼看一看，塔米拉小姐在舞台下的样子呢？
t_en: Could you please go and see for yourself how Tamila looks when she's offstage?

t_ch: 小女子听说塔米拉小姐今天会去买书，拜托你去书摊看看她是怎样买书的吧~谢谢你啦~
t_en: I heard that she's going to buy some more books today. Please, can you go to the bookstore and observe how she buys her books? Thank you so much~

t_ch: 好久不见了，菲丝，最近有什么好书可以推荐吗？
t_en: Long time no see, Fizz! Have you got any book recommendations for me today?

t_ch: 唔，如果是像《歌德宾馆的夜与雾》那样的书…恐怕我还没找到适合你的……
t_en: Well, if it's something similar to Evening Ember at the Goethe Hotel that you're after, I'm afraid I've got nothing for you...

t_ch: 不，没关系，我到也不是非要看那样的小说…最近我也想突破一下舒适圈，看看以前没读过的类型，也许就会打开新世界的大门呢。
t_en: No, that's okay. I don't really have to read that kind of novel... I've decided to break out of my comfort zone recently and delve into some genres that I haven't read before. Perhaps it'll open up a pathway to a whole new world for me.

t_ch: 这样的话…我听说《雪国冒险奇谭》可能要解禁了，到时你可以读一读。
t_en: If that's the case... I heard the Tale of the Winterlands might be getting unbanned. You can read it then.

t_ch: 呃…这本还是算了吧，之前花店那个小姑娘，拉着我讲了一下午这本书的故事。
t_en: Ugh... I'll pass on that one. The last time when I was at the florist, she chewed my ear off all day about it.

t_ch: 但她的叙述真的颠三倒四，最后我只记住了阿尔乔姆和什么炽烈且真挚的爱情……搞得我有点心理阴影。
t_en: But her description was all over the place. All I remember is someone called Artem and his blazing sincere love... it made me kind of gloomy.

t_ch: …嗯？我们看的真的是同一本《雪国冒险奇谭》吗？
t_en: ...Hmmm? Are we talking about the same Tale of the Winterlands here!?

t_ch: 关于塔米拉的一切…
t_en: Everything about Tamila...

t_ch: 喔喔，原来如此…小女子对塔米拉小姐的理解更深了~
t_en: Ooh, I see... I have a deeper understanding of Tamila now.

t_ch: 谢谢你~这是给你的谢礼~
t_en: Thank you. Here's a token of my appreciation!

t_ch: 「陛下、陛下~你应当遮蔽世间的明镜~连同平静的湖水~你需道出她的真名实姓~驱逐千面的妖妃~」
t_en: "Your Highness, Your Highness~! You must shield yourself from all mirrors of the world, even the serene lakes! You must ask for her true name and banish that duplicitous succubus!"

t_ch: 小女子听说塔米拉小姐今天会去买花，拜托你去花店看看她是怎样买花的吧~谢谢你啦~
t_en: I heard that she's going to buy some more flowers today. Please, can you go to the florist and observe how she buys her bouquets? Thank you so much~

t_ch: 塔米拉小姐，今天也要七彩虹吗？
t_en: Hi Tamila, are you after more Rainbows today?

t_ch: 嗯，先帮我打包三十束吧。
t_en: Mmm, please wrap up thirty bouquets for me.

t_ch: 需要我们送到府上吗？
t_en: Do you need us to deliver it to your residence?

t_ch: 给我一束吧，剩下的帮我送到家里，谢谢。
t_en: Give one bouquet to me now, and send the rest to my place. Thank you.

t_ch: 塔米拉小姐是忍不住要立刻带一束花回家吗？真浪漫啊…只买最平凡的花这一点，也好浪漫啊…我不由得联想到了阿尔……
t_en: Are you that eager to take one bunch with you on the way home? That's so romantic... Just like buying ordinary flowers like this is romantic, too... I can't help but think of Arte...

t_ch: 停！你要是再趁机开始拉着我讲那本小说，我就再也不来你这儿买花了。
t_en: Stop right there! If you try telling me about that blasted book one more time, I'm never coming here to buy your flowers ever again.

t_ch: 呜…好吧。塔米拉小姐慢走，塔米拉小姐再来……
t_en: Oh... okay. So long, and see you again soon...

t_en: I heard that she's going for a walk in the plaza today. Please, can you go to the plaza and observe what she's up to? Thank you so much~

t_ch: 每次站在这个地方都觉得不可思议，人真是顽强的动物。
t_en: I find it incredible every time I come here. Humans are such resilient animals.

t_ch: 如果有什么异世界的人来到贝洛伯格，他们一定会觉得「这里不适宜人类居住」。
t_en: If an alien came to Belobog, they'd definitely think "This place is unfit for human habitation."

t_ch: 可如果真的站在贝洛伯格的广场上，却会发现…任何一个人类勉强可以居住的地方，都会变得生机勃勃。
t_en: But if you stand in the Belobog plaza and observe, you'd realize that... Humans have the power to turn even the barely habitable places into a land of exuberance.

t_ch: 看看这些人吧。贵族和官员、小贩和工人、满嘴胡话的笨蛋和不苟言笑的天才…他们都是我的养料。
t_en: Just look at these people. Aristocrats and bureaucrats, merchants and workers, vocal fools and mirthless geniuses... they're all nourishment for me.

t_ch: 只是站在这里，我就感到自己对演技的理解更深了。
t_en: Just standing here helps me understand my art just a little bit deeper.

t_ch: 没有人可以取代这样的我…没有人。
t_en: Nobody can replace me when I'm like this... nobody.

t_en: I heard that she's going to the workshop today. Please, can you go to the workshop and observe what she's up to? Thank you so much~

t_ch: 喔，贵客啊。塔米拉小姐，今天有什么事吗？
t_en: Oh, a customer. Tamila! What's up today?

t_ch: 倒是没什么，就是想起来…你好像也是个歌手吧？
t_en: Nothing much. I just thought... you're a singer too, aren't you?

t_ch: 和你这种大明星还是没法比啦，最多算是爱好。
t_en: I'm nothing compared to a big star like you! It's just a hobby of mine.

t_ch: 别这么说，能不能用来赚钱，可不是评价艺术水平优劣的标准。
t_en: Don't say that! Good art isn't determined by whether or not you can make money off of it.

t_en: Recently, I've been wondering whether or not this bottleneck I seem to have reached is due to being limited by my singing style...

t_ch: 所以我想找你交流一下…也许你那些摇滚乐的发声技巧，能给我一些新的突破呢。
t_en: That's why I came to chat with you... Maybe your rock music vocals can help me make a breakthrough to a higher level.

t_ch: 好说，好说。跟我来后台吧，咱们交流一下。
t_en: Interesting. Come backstage with me, and let's have a chat.

t_ch: 不过不要抱太大期望哦~毕竟隔行如隔山…对吧。

t_ch: 没关系啦，你肯帮我，我已经很感谢了。
t_en: Don't say that. The fact that you're willing to help me is already helpful in itself.

t_ch: 能不能请你亲眼看一看，塔米拉小姐在舞台下的样子呢？
t_en: Could you please go and see for yourself how Tamila looks when she's offstage?

t_ch: 小女子听说塔米拉小姐今天要去歌德宾馆，拜托你去歌德宾馆看看她是去做什么吧~谢谢你啦~
t_en: I heard that she's going to the Goethe Hotel today. Please, can you go to the hotel and observe what she's up to? Thank you so much~

t_ch: 这不是塔米拉小姐吗？稀客啊。您是要住店还是要喝杯下午茶呢？
t_en: Is that Tamila? A rare blessing. Would you like to stay in the hotel, or perhaps a pot of tea?

t_ch: 嗯…这里和想象中的氛围不太一样啊。
t_en: Hmm... the ambiance here is not what I expected.

t_ch: 想象中的氛围？
t_en: What did you expect?

t_ch: 不，没什么，只是觉得这里不像书中的气氛。
t_en: Oh, it's nothing. It's just different from how it's described in the book.

t_ch: 书中的气氛？抱歉，我没太明白……
t_en: The book? I'm sorry. I don't quite understand...

t_ch: 没什么，歌德先生，这同样很好。请给我来一杯下午茶吧。
t_en: It's really nothing, Mr. Goethe. This is fine as well. I'll have a cup of tea, please.

t_ch: …噢噢，好的，我这就去安排。您要坐在哪里？
t_en: ...Ah, all right then. I shall get right on it. Where would you like to sit?

t_ch: 就那里吧，靠窗的位置。那里阳光正好，一定很舒服。
t_en: I'll sit over there, by the window. I'm sure the sunlight there will make me warm and comfy.

t_ch: 好嘞~您稍等。
t_en: Right then, a moment, please.

t_ch: 关于塔米拉的一切…
t_en: Everything about Tamila...

t_ch: 喔喔，原来如此…小女子对塔米拉小姐的理解更深了~
t_en: Ooh, I see... I have a deeper understanding of Tamila now.

t_ch: 谢谢你~这是给你的谢礼~
t_en: Thank you. Here's a token of my appreciation!

t_ch: 「陛下、陛下~你应当遮蔽世间的明镜~连同平静的湖水~你需道出她的真名实姓~驱逐千面的妖妃~」
t_en: "Your Highness, Your Highness~! You must shield yourself from all mirrors of the world, even the serene lakes! You must ask for her true name and banish that duplicitous succubus!"

t_ch: 你好像有事想找人帮忙？
t_en: You seem to need help with something?

t_ch: 帮忙？哈哈，像你这样热心肠的年轻人在贝洛伯格可真少见。
t_en: Help? Hahaha, it's rare to see helpful kids like you in Belobog.

t_ch: 不过，你说得对——确实有件要紧事。只是，我不知道该不该托付给你……
t_en: You're right, though. There is something urgent I need help with. I just don't know whether I should ask for your help...

t_ch: 尽管交给我吧！
t_en: Just leave it to me!

t_ch: 哈哈，既然你这么说，那我可不能再谢绝你的好意了。
t_en: Hahaha. I won't refuse your help then.

t_ch: 难道其中暗藏玄机？
t_en: Is there any funny business in this?

t_ch: 哈哈，年轻人的悟性就是高啊。
t_en: Hahaha, you young people are pretty sharp.

t_ch: 嗯…此事让你了解也无妨，我就把它交给你吧。
t_en: Mmm... It doesn't hurt to let you know. I'll leave this to you.

t_ch: 长话短说——今天我原本与人约好在此地碰头，那人说要借宾馆房间一用。
t_en: Long story short — I am supposed to meet someone here today, as they wanted to borrow a hotel room.

t_ch: 结果，现在距约定的时间已过去许久，他人还迟迟未出现…而我得守着这柜台，不方便离开，也无法前去查看他的状况。
t_en: But it's been long past the time of our appointment, and he's still not here yet. I need to look after the front counter and can't leave, so I can't go see what is happening.

t_ch: 所以，我想拜托你代我前去看看他那里究竟发生了什么事。尽管放心，我会给你合适的报酬的。
t_en: Therefore, I want to ask you to go see what happened to him. Rest assured, I will give you a suitable reward.

t_ch: 我该去找谁？
t_en: Who should I find?

t_ch: 罗希。他平时闲得没事就爱在宾馆大堂转悠…逢人便逮住讨论时事要闻。
t_en: Rossy. He likes to wander around in the hotel lobby whenever he has time... And discuss current affairs with anyone he sees.

t_ch: 不出意外的话，你可以在那里找到他。
t_en: You should be able to find him there.

t_ch: 就只是看看？
t_en: Am I just going to SEE what is happening?

t_ch: 对，就只是看看。你只要告诉他「我是会社派来的」，他就明白你的来意了。
t_en: Yes, just there to see. Tell him that you're "from the Society" and he'll understand.

t_ch: 确认过后，还得麻烦你回来向我说明一下情况…剩下的由我来接手即可。
t_en: Please come back to let me know of the situation once you're done, and I'll handle the rest.

t_ch: 明白了，我这就去。
t_en: Understood. I'm heading over now.

t_ch: 别忘了——要找的人是罗希，他平时闲得没事就爱在宾馆大堂转悠…你应该可以轻松找到他。
t_en: Don't forget — You are looking for Rossy, who likes to wander around in the hotel lobby whenever he has time... You should be able to find him easily.

t_ch: 如果他不明白你的来意，只需要告诉他「我是会社派来的」，他会懂的。
t_en: If he doesn't understand why you're there, just tell him that you're "from the Society" and he'll catch on.

t_ch: 那么就拜托你为我跑一趟了，实在不好意思。
t_en: Well then, please do this errand for me. Thank you so much.

t_ch: 你小子又想上哪儿去？
t_en: Where are you trying to go, boy?

t_ch: 我…我就是去做义工！
t_en: I—I'm going to do volunteer work!

t_ch: 气死我了——你这臭小子又想上哪儿去？
t_en: I'm so annoyed — Where are you trying to go, boy!?

t_ch: 我…我就是去做义工！
t_en: I—I'm going to do volunteer work!

t_ch: 哎，真是的…我都多大了，又不是小屁孩！你至于管得这么严吗，我又不可能偷偷看那什么《奇谭》……
t_en: Seriously... I'm not a child anymore! You can't tell me what to do all the time. It's not like I'm gonna go read that Tales series...

t_ch: 哈，我就知道！你小子就是想找地方看禁书！
t_en: Ha, I knew it! You're just trying to find a spot to read that banned book!

t_ch: 哈，你来得正好！来来来，你来给我们父子俩评评理！
t_en: Ha, you're here at a good time! Come and tell me what you think about my son's actions!

t_ch: 我是会社派来的。
t_en: I'm from the Society.

t_ch: 哎呦喂，谢天谢地！可算是有个自己人了……
t_en: Oh, thank goodness! Finally, someone on my side...

t_ch: 不了吧，清官都难断家务事啊。
t_en: I'll pass. Every family has its own problems.

t_ch: 咳咳，也是。让你看我们父子俩闹这么一出实在是不好意思啊。你先走吧，我们自己解决……
t_en: *cough* You're right. I... I shouldn't let you see this debacle between my son and I. Go ahead and leave. We can deal with this ourselves.

t_ch: 哎哎哎？！等下，你别走！我看你从宾馆前台那边来…难不成你和老歌德聊过了？
t_en: Hey! Wait! Don't go! I saw you came from the hotel's reception desk... Did you talk to Old Goethe?

t_ch: 是啊，我是会社派来的。
t_en: Yes, I'm from the Society.

t_ch: 哎呦喂，谢天谢地！可算是有个自己人了……
t_en: Oh, thank goodness! Finally, someone on my side...

t_ch: 我不是，别乱说。
t_en: I didn't. Don't jump to conclusions.

t_ch: 嗐，甭管那么多了——今天你不是也得是！是不是「会社」喊我去做义工？老爹，你看，我说什么来着？
t_en: Oh, who cares — You are helping me whether you like it or not! Is the Society asking me to do volunteer work? Dad, see? I told you!

t_ch: 你们先吵，我就凑个热闹。
t_en: Keep arguing. I'm just here to watch.

t_ch: 哎，等会！我我我…我知道你是替「会社」来喊人的！
t_en: Hey, wait! I—I know you're here to find people for the Society!

t_ch: 我这就来、这就来——你赶紧带我走！赶紧！
t_en: I'm coming. I'm coming — Hurry and take me away. Quickly!

t_ch: 什么…「会社」？好小子，在外边加入什么乱七八糟的组织了？！
t_en: What society? Did you join some shady organization, boy?

t_ch: 不不不，我都说了是去做义工啊！我们这个「会社」是……
t_en: No no no, I told you I'm going to do volunteer work! This Society, it's...

t_ch: 呃…是……
t_en: Um, it's...

t_ch: 就是志愿者团体！
t_en: It's a volunteer group!

t_ch: 对对对，就是志愿者团体！
t_en: Yes, a volunteer group!

t_ch: 是《雪国冒险奇谭》书友会！
t_en: It's the book club for Tale of the Winterlands!

t_ch: 罗希啊罗希，你小子就给我嘴硬，这下让我抓到现行了吧！
t_en: Rossy! You keep denying this, but I caught you in the act!

t_ch: 哎…这都哪跟哪啊！我们这是志愿者团体——专门做义工、做好事的！
t_en: Huh... Wait, no! We are a volunteer group — We volunteer and do good deeds!

t_ch: 到底是什么呢？
t_en: What exactly do we do?

t_ch: 是什么？你说啊？
t_en: What do you do? Spell it out!

t_ch: 哎…你别添乱！我们这是志愿者团体——专门做义工、做好事的！
t_en: Hey, don't make it worse! We are a volunteer group — We volunteer and do good deeds!

t_ch: 难不成真是…？
t_en: Could it be true...?

t_ch: 哎哟，我还能骗您不成啊？我虽然说话不中听，但您看我从小到大什么时候骗过您？
t_en: Oh, come on, do you think I'm lying? I may talk trash, but I've never lied to you.

t_ch: 啧…算了。这次就信你一回吧，下不为例！
t_en: Tsk... Never mind. I'll believe you this once!

t_ch: 嘿，这事就算是解决咯。感谢老爹！
t_en: Hehe, well this is as good as done. Thank you, dad!

t_ch: 呃…那个啥，你是叫{NICKNAME}是吧？谢谢你替我解围。你要是真认识老歌德的话，能帮我跟他转达下情况不？就说我晚点去找他。
t_en: Um... You are {NICKNAME}, right? Thank you for helping me out. If you know Old Goethe, can you tell him what happened? Tell him I'll see him later.

t_ch: 没问题。
t_en: No problem.

t_ch: 嘿嘿，多谢！
t_en: Hehe, thank you!

t_ch: 看我心情…
t_en: It depends on my mood...

t_ch: 成，都听你的！你是我的大恩人……
t_en: Alright, I'll listen to you! You helped me so much...

t_ch: 你怎么还不去做义工？
t_en: Why haven't you gone to your volunteer work yet?

t_ch: 嗐，其实我刚才正等人来送书呢——我想看的那册《奇谭》家里正好没有，非得从别人手里借才行……
t_en: Heh, I was actually waiting for someone to give me the book — I don't have the specific book in the Tales series that I wanted to read, so I had to burrow it...

t_ch: 结果我老爹半道杀出，那人估计见状跑得没影了…你说说吧，这都办的什么事啊！害得我又得浪费时间等那人带着书回来……
t_en: Then my dad popped out of nowhere, and the book owner probably ran away... Seriously, what is this mess? Now I have to waste time waiting for the person to bring the book back...

t_ch: 哎，无论如何你救了我一命，多谢啦！等拿到书我就去找老歌德碰头——不过你要是先见到他的话，还得请你拜托帮忙传话了。
t_en: Anyways, thank you for saving me! I'll be heading to Old Goethe's once I get the book — But if you see him first, please let him know.

t_ch: 罗希的事情摆平了。
t_en: I solved Rossy's issue.

t_ch: 摆平了？什么意思？
t_en: Solved? What do you mean?

t_ch: 你将方才的遭遇一五一十地报告了老歌德……
t_en: You tell Old Goethe what happened...

t_ch: 哈哈哈…没想到还有这种事啊。不过，这也正是我们「会社」存在的意义。
t_en: Hahaha... I didn't think this would happen, but that is indeed why the Society exists.

t_ch: 所以这「会社」到底是什么？
t_en: So, what exactly is the Society?

t_ch: 嗯…既然你帮了我们的忙，那你也是我们的一份子了。我就简单给你介绍一下吧。
t_en: Well, since you helped us, you are now one of us. I'll give you a short introduction.

t_ch: 我们的「会社」全称「正当权益会社」——顾名思义，我们的目标便是维护每个人的「正当权益」。
t_en: Our Society is called The Rightful Rights Society — As the name implies, our goal is to protect everyone's rightful rights.

t_ch: 简单来说，就是让每个人都能暂时逃离家庭和责任的束缚，自由自在地发展自己的兴趣爱好、或者只是单纯地休息一下……
t_en: In short, we can get everyone to temporarily escape from the bindings of family and duty, to freely develop their hobbies and preferences, or simply to rest...

t_ch: 总之，我们是个造福社会的公益组织，而我老歌德正是行政区分社的负责人。我很欣赏你的热情和能力——有兴趣加入我们吗？
t_en: Anyways, we're a charitable organization that aims to make the world a better place, and I'm the manager for the Administrative District branch. I like your passion and capacity. Would you be interested in joining us?

t_ch: 听着不像公益组织啊…
t_en: It doesn't sound like a charitable organization.

t_ch: 哈哈，公益组织不就是义务为人做好事嘛。
t_en: Hahaha, aren't charitable organizations all about doing good deeds for free?

t_ch: 从这个角度出发，我们「正当权益会社」显然是个公益组织。
t_en: If you view it that way, then The Rightful Rights Society is certainly a charitable organization.

t_ch: 原来你还有这种隐藏身份。
t_en: So, this is your hidden identity.

t_ch: 没错。毕竟我们平时都需要隐秘行动，所以不能在外声张自己的身份——这也是我最初请你帮忙时有所犹豫的原因。
t_en: That's right. We usually act in secrecy and don't announce our identities, which is why I hesitated to ask for your help.

t_ch: 不过，现在你已经知道了「会社」和我的身份…这意味着我们已经准备好接纳你，现在只看你如何回应了。
t_en: However, since you know about my identity and the Society, we are now ready to accept you into our ranks. The choice is up to you.

t_ch: 加入之后有什么好处？
t_en: What are the benefits of joining?

t_ch: 嗯，这是个不错的问题。
t_en: Mmm, good question.

t_ch: 首先，如果你感到自己需要一些休闲时间却脱不开身，你就可以求助社友，拜托他们替你打掩护；其次，你也可以在有空时协助配合其他成员。
t_en: Firstly, if you ever feel you need some time to relax but can't get away from your duties, you can ask other Society members to help cover for you. Secondly, you can also cover for other members whenever you can.

t_ch: 当然，这并不意味着你可以随便差使社友…要想享受权利，你就必须有过帮助他人的记录才行。
t_en: This certainly doesn't mean you get to command other Society members as you wish. To enjoy the benefits of the Society, you need to have a record of having helped others.

t_ch: 一次帮助可抵两次求助——我们鼓励大家相互支援，一起享受休闲时光。
t_en: One instance of helping other Members equals two instances of asking for help. We encourage everyone to help each other and enjoy some off time.

t_ch: 不加入的话，你会放我走吗？
t_en: Are you going to let me go if I refuse to join?

t_ch: 呵呵，不想加入吗？那么我会给你开个无法拒绝的条件……
t_en: Ho-ho, you don't want to join? Then I will give you an offer you cannot refuse...

t_ch: …开个玩笑。加入「会社」与否是你的自由——我们不会逼迫你的。
t_en: ...I'm joking. You're free to join the Society or not. We won't force you.

t_ch: 有意思，我加入。
t_en: Sounds interesting. I'm in.

t_ch: 哈哈，那可真是太好了！那么，你就是我们「会社」的一份子了。
t_en: Hahaha, good! You're now a member of the Society.

t_ch: 如果其他社友发布了委托，我就会通知到你。你可以自行判断是否提供协助。
t_en: I'll contact you when other members put up commissions, and you can decide whether to help them.

t_ch: 另外…这是今天罗希一事的报酬——请你笑纳。
t_en: And this is the reward for Rossy's business today — with warm regards.

t_ch: 我来帮你行使正当权益！
t_en: I'll help you exercise your rightful rights!

t_ch: 哇，没想到这么快就有人来啦！那我就和你说说我的需求吧。
t_en: Woah, I didn't expect someone to arrive so quickly! Let me tell you what I need.

t_ch: 你也知道我是《水晶日报》美食专栏的记者吧？最近我在创作上遇到了一些瓶颈，脑子简直是一团浆糊了。
t_en: You know I'm a column journalist for Crystal Daily, right? I've hit a bit of a bottleneck with my writing recently. My brain is all a fog.

t_ch: 但你也知道，我们这种工作单位就爱抓考勤…早上一次，中午一次，晚上走的时候还得来一次——连个逃离办公室换换脑子的机会都没有，我真是受够了！
t_en: But companies like ours love to check for attendance... They check once in the morning, once at noon, and once at night! I can't even step outside the office to relax my brain! I've had enough!

t_ch: 所以我就想请两天病假休息一下。但我们主编又说请病假必须提供医生证明…实在没办法，我只好通过「会社」发布委托了。
t_en: So, I want to take a break and get a couple of days of sick leave, but the chief editor says I need a doctor's letter... I'm all out of ideas, which is why I put up a Society commission.

t_ch: 我的需求就是这样啦。你可以接受吗？不行的话我就再等等别人好了……
t_en: Well, that's my task. Do you want to take it? If you don't, I'll just wait for someone else...

t_ch: 小事一桩，我正好有个朋友…
t_en: Piece of cake. I've got a friend.

t_ch: 难道…你那朋友正好是个医生？那可是帮大忙啦！
t_en: Is your friend a doctor? That's awesome!

t_ch: 我认识的医生在下层区…
t_en: This doctor friend of mine is in the Underworld...

t_ch: 咦？你有认识的医生吗？还是在下层区？！
t_en: Huh? You know a doctor? In the Underworld!?

t_ch: 不知道我那主编认不认下层区的医生证明——尤其是这种特殊时期……
t_en: I don't know if my chief editor would recognize an Underworlder doctor's practicing license — Especially during sensitive times like this...

t_ch: 但…还是请你帮忙问问看吧。兴许他发现不了呢！
t_en: But... please ask your friend for a favor anyways! Maybe the chief editor won't notice!

t_ch: 我就在这里等你的好消息——拜托你啦！
t_en: I'll be waiting right here for your good news — Please!

t_ch: 想请问你能否开具医生证明…
t_en: Can you write a sick leave letter...?

t_ch: 你将艾格尼斯的请求转告了娜塔莎……
t_en: Telling Natasha about Agnes' request...

t_ch: 呵呵…「正当权益会社」啊，真是个久违了的名字。
t_en: Hahaha... The Rightful Rights Society. I haven't heard that name for a long time.

t_ch: 出于职业道德，我一般是不会随便给人开具证明的…但「会社」的大家都有苦衷，这点我也明白。
t_en: I usually don't write such letters because of professional ethics... But I know everyone in the Society has their own reasons.

t_ch: 这张证明就给你吧——但我可不保证它真能帮那位艾格尼斯小姐蒙混过关哦。
t_en: Here's the letter. No guarantees this will pass any detailed scrutiny, though.

t_ch: 我帮你开好医生证明了。
t_en: I got you a doctor's letter.

t_ch: 真的吗？！谢谢你！快让我看看……
t_en: Really!? Thank you so much! Let me see...
Um...

t_ch: 这…因在矿井长时间劳作造成腰间盘突出…？这个真的有用吗……
t_en: L—Lumbar disc herniation due to prolonged manual labor in the mines...? Is this excuse going to work?

t_ch: 算了算了，不管了！能开到证明就是胜利——我就去赌一把吧！辛苦你为我跑一趟啦。
t_en: Never mind. I don't care anymore! Having a letter is good enough. I'm gonna go take my chances! Thank you for doing this for me.

t_ch: 如果你也需要帮助的话，就通过老歌德发布委托吧。我看到你委托的话，一定会第一时间前来帮忙的——
t_en: If you ever need any help, you can send out a commission with Old Goethe, too. If I see your commission, I'll rush over to help as soon as I can —

t_ch: 还有…这是我的一点心意，请收下吧！
t_en: And... this is a small reward. Please take it!

t_ch: 你在告示板的角落里发现了这样一张纸条——
t_en: You found a note in a corner of the notice board that says:

t_ch: 「正当权益会社——会员 艾格尼斯 发布了一则委托。有意向提供协助的社友请前往面洽！」
t_en: "Agnes, a member of the Rightful Rights Society, has issued an assignment. Please contact Agnes if you are interested in offering your help!"

t_ch: （去找艾格尼斯问问看吧……）
t_en: (I'll ask Agnes about it...)

t_ch: 我来帮你行使正当权益！
t_en: I'll help you exercise your rightful rights!

t_ch: 哦，是你。看来这世上还是好心人多…我放心了。
t_en: Oh, it's you. Seems like there are still plenty of good people in this world... That's good.

t_ch: 我的状况…是这样的。我本是《雪国冒险奇谭》续作代笔，可我实在没办法模仿出原作的韵味……
t_en: My situation... is this. I'm the ghostwriter of the sequel for Tale of the Winterlands, but I just can't manage to copy the feelings of the original...

t_ch: 也正因此，有位编辑正在追着我讨债…她最近似乎发现了我的住所，便连日登门拜访。可我哪里有稿子能拿得出手？
t_en: That's why an editor is hot on my trail... She recently found out where I live and keeps visiting me, but I don't have any manuscripts to show her!

t_ch: 那位编辑…名叫尼古莱。而我的需求就是请人把她支走——什么说法、什么方式都可以，最好能让她以为亚诺这人已经永远消失了……
t_en: That editor is named Nika, and my commission to you is to get her away from me — I don't care how you do it or what you say to her. It'd be best if she thinks Amo is gone and never coming back.

t_ch: 怎么样，你能做到吗？要是不行的话，我得再想想其他办法……
t_en: Can you do that? If you can't, then I'll think of something else...

t_ch: 交给我吧，我和她说。
t_en: Leave it to me. I'll talk to her.

t_ch: 我可太会说服人了，放心吧！
t_en: I'm very good at persuading people! Leave it to me!

t_ch: 听到你这么说，我心里竟然一沉……
t_en: Now that you've said that, I'm even more concerned...

t_ch: 对不起，可能是我太悲观了。请你别放在心上。
t_en: Sorry. Maybe I'm being too pessimistic. Don't take it to heart.

t_ch: 那就拜托了，感激不尽！
t_en: Please! You have my sincerest thanks!

t_ch: 亚诺他…
t_en: About Amo...

t_ch: 哦？你认识那位大作家啊，他怎么了？
t_en: Oh? You know that famous author? What about him?

t_ch: 他说他写不出来，交不了稿。
t_en: He says he's having trouble writing and can't hand in the manuscript.

t_ch: 哈哈，原来是这样。我说最近怎么找不到他人……
t_en: Hahaha, I see. I was wondering why I couldn't find him lately...

t_ch: 他消失在了雪原深处…
t_en: He disappeared into the depth of the Snow Plains...

t_ch: 原来是这样。我说最近怎么找不到他人……
t_en: I see. I was wondering why I couldn't find him lately...

t_ch: …哈哈，开玩笑，我才不会相信这种借口。你还不如说他翻垃圾桶翻出病了呢。
t_en: ...Yeah, right. I'm not going to believe such an excuse. You might as well tell me he got sick from going through Belobog's trashcans.

t_ch: 好吧，被你识破了。
t_en: Alright. You saw through me.

t_ch: 没关系。你是来替他求情的，对吧？
t_en: It's okay. You're here to ask me to leave him alone, right?

t_ch: 他确实因为翻垃圾桶生病了…
t_en: He did get sick from going through the trashcans...

t_ch: …倒也不必顺着我的话往下说。我知道，你是来替他求情的，对吧？
t_en: ...You didn't have to run with what I said. I know you're here to ask me to leave him alone, right?

t_ch: 他真的去雪原了，我会骗你吗？
t_en: He really did go to the Snow Plains. I'm serious.

t_ch: 好了好了，别演了——你是来替他求情的，对吧？
t_en: Alright alright, you can stop now — You're here to ask me to leave him alone, right?

t_ch: 你让他放心吧，我最近找他的原因根本就不是催稿。其实是我们出版社主编看中了他的才能，想要聘请他当《水晶日报》的专栏作者呢。
t_en: Tell him not to worry. I'm not looking for him because of the manuscript. It's because my chief editor likes his work and wants to hire him as a column writer for the Crystal Daily.

t_ch: 不过他总是躲着我，这些话也没办法传达给他了。如果你见到他的话，可以麻烦你帮忙转达一下吗？
t_en: But since he is always hiding from me, I never got to tell him about this. Could you please let him know when you see him?

t_ch: 如果他有兴趣的话，不妨让他来与我社当面商议。主编似乎很中意他，条件什么的都好商量——如果能成，他也不用每天抱着垃圾桶唉声叹气了吧……
t_en: If he's interested, he can come directly to our office for a discussion. The chief editor really likes his work and our terms are negotiable — He won't have to sigh all day while hugging a trashcan anymore...

t_ch: 尼古莱这么说…
t_en: This is what Nika said.

t_ch: 你将尼古莱的话转告了亚诺……
t_en: You told Amo what Nika had said...

t_ch: …原来是这样。
t_en: ...So that's how it is.

t_ch: 这是个陷阱，我不会上当的。她肯定是想当面拷打我，把我关进小黑屋，逼我出稿……
t_en: This is a trap. I won't fall for it. She just wants to find and torture me, lock me up, and force me to write...

t_ch: 我绝不会离开我的垃圾桶！只有这里才是我的归宿……
t_en: I'm never leaving my trashcan! This is my only home...

t_ch: 啊…抱歉。我只顾着自怜自艾，都忘记向你道谢了。
t_en: Ah... I'm sorry. I was so engrossed in feeling sorry for myself that I forgot to thank you.

t_ch: 谢谢你为我跑腿，这是我仅有的部分积蓄，请你收下……
t_en: Thank you for running the errand for me. These are my only savings. Please take them...

t_ch: 你在告示板的角落里发现了这样一张纸条——
t_en: You found a note in a corner of the notice board that says:

t_ch: 「正当权益会社——会员 亚诺 发布了一则委托。有意向提供协助的社友请前往面洽！」
t_en: "Amo, a member of the Rightful Rights Society, has issued an assignment. Please contact Amo if you are interested in offering your help!"

t_ch: （去找亚诺问问看吧……）
t_en: (I'll ask Amo about it...)

t_ch: 我来帮你行使正当权益！
t_en: I'll help you exercise your rightful rights!

t_ch: 喔，没想到这么快就有社友来帮忙了！看来多多加入公益组织是个正确的决定。
t_en: Oh, I didn't think other Society members would be here so soon! Seems like it's a good decision to join charitable organizations.

t_ch: 我的需求是——希望你能为我拍张照！就这样。
t_en: I need you to — take a photo of me! That's all.

t_ch: 好啊，没问题。
t_en: Alright. No problem.

t_ch: 喔，真爽快！那就拜托你了，记得帮我拍得英俊、高大一点——这可是要给我女儿看的！
t_en: Oh, nice! Please, make me look tall and handsome — This photo is for my daughter.

t_ch: 好啊，但为什么？
t_en: Okay, but why?

t_ch: 哈哈，先拍照吧，拍完我就告诉你。
t_en: Hahaha, just take the photo. I'll tell you afterwards.

t_ch: 好吧，那你摆个姿势。
t_en: Okay. Please make a pose.

t_ch: 那就拜托你了，记得帮我拍得英俊、高大一点——这可是要给我女儿看的！
t_en: Please, make me look tall and handsome — This photo is for my daughter.

t_ch: 不愧是受害者互助会的老大…
t_en: You are certainly the leader of the Scam Victims Association...

t_ch: 吃一堑长一智嘛，留点心眼总是好的！
t_en: I'm just being smart. It's good to be on your guard around other people!

t_ch: 那么，照片就拜托你了。记得帮我拍得英俊、高大一点——这可是要给我女儿看的！
t_en: The camera is all yours! Please, make me look tall and handsome — This photo is for my daughter.

t_ch: 拍完了？多谢！那就来让我看看……
t_en: You're done? Thank you! Let me have a look...

t_ch: 哎呀，这拍得真不错！看起来就像我年轻了十岁，哈哈哈。
t_en: Oh, this is a good photo! I look ten years younger in this, hahaha.

t_ch: 那就先这样？
t_en: So, this is it?

t_ch: 好啊好啊，谢谢你帮忙！这是我的一点心意……
t_en: Yes, thank you for your help! This is a small token of thanks...

t_ch: …呃，总感觉忘了点什么事。是什么来着？
t_en: ...Um, I feel like I forgot something. What could it be?

t_ch: 哎，算了算了，回头想起来再说吧。总之请收下我的谢礼！
t_en: Never mind. I'll get to it when I remember. Anyways, please accept my thanks!

t_ch: 现在能说说为什么要拍照了吧？
t_en: Can you tell me now why you want a photo?

t_ch: 哈哈，这可说来话长了。我想想怎么组织语言……
t_en: Hahaha, that's a long story. Let me think about where to start...

t_ch: 事情是这样——你也知道我每天都专注于推广「深蓝骗局反诈技术」，对吧？
t_en: Okay — You know I spend my time every day promoting techniques against Dark-Blue Scams, right?

t_ch: 我经常在广场上一站就是一天，有时候很晚才回去。结果，我家女儿就不乐意了，非说我不愿意陪她玩；其他家人也怀疑我在外面做些不三不四的事……
t_en: Sometimes I stay out in the Plaza all day and only get home late at night. My daughter got upset and said I don't want to play with her, and my family also thinks I'm doing some shady business out here...

t_ch: 于是我就想到了拍照这个点子——这样就能证明我确实是在外面工作了！
t_en: So I thought about taking a photo to prove that I'm in fact working during the day!

t_ch: 家家有本难念的经啊。
t_en: Every family has its own troubles.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，其实都是小事！我们一家子关系其实还不错。
t_en: Hahaha, it's nothing! My family actually gets on very well.

t_ch: 「会社」不该帮人逃避工作吗？
t_en: Isn't the Society all about helping people avoid work?

t_ch: 这么理解可就太片面了！你想啊，「正当权益会社」是帮助人行使「正当权益」的，而我的工作则是确保别人的「正当权益」不受侵犯……
t_en: That's not the whole picture! Think about it — The Rightful Rights Society helps people to exercise their rightful rights, and my job is to ensure people's rights are not violated...

t_ch: 没错，这和「会社」的价值观是不冲突的——「正当权益会社」还是做了它该做的事，没毛病吧？
t_en: There is no conflict between my job and the mission of the Society. The Rightful Rights Society is still doing its thing.

t_ch: 噢哟，不小心扯远了。总之，谢谢你帮忙！这是我的一点心意……
t_en: Anyways, that went off on a tangent. Thank you! These are your rewards...

t_ch: 拍完了？多谢！那就来让我看看……
t_en: You're done? Thank you! Let me have a look...

t_ch: 哎，我人呢？看不见啊，在哪儿呢…这样的照片好像并不能证明我的清白啊。
t_en: Huh, where am I in this photo? I can't see myself in this... This picture is not gonna prove my claims.

t_ch: 不过算了，毕竟你特意跑一趟，我再胡乱提要求也不合适。谢谢你啊。
t_en: Never mind. You came all the way out here for this, and I can't ask too much from you. Thank you.

t_ch: 那就先这样？
t_en: So, this is it?

t_ch: 好啊好啊，谢谢你帮忙！这是我的一点心意……
t_en: Yes, thank you for your help! This is a small token of thanks...

t_ch: …呃，总感觉忘了点什么事。是什么来着？
t_en: ...Um, I feel like I forgot something. What could it be?

t_ch: 哎，算了算了，回头想起来再说吧。总之请收下我的谢礼！
t_en: Never mind. I'll get to it when I remember. Anyways, please accept my thanks!

t_ch: 现在能说说为什么要拍照了吧？
t_en: Can you tell me now why you want a photo?

t_ch: 哈哈，这可说来话长了。我想想怎么组织语言……
t_en: Hahaha, that's a long story. Let me think about where to start...

t_ch: 其实事情是这样的——你也知道我每天都专注于推广「深蓝骗局反诈技术」，对吧？
t_en: Okay — You know I spend my time every day promoting techniques against Dark-Blue Scams, right?

t_ch: 我在广场上经常一站就是一天，有时候到很晚才回去。结果，我家女儿就不乐意了，非说我不愿意陪她玩；其他家人也怀疑我在外面做些不三不四的事……
t_en: Sometimes I stay out in the Plaza all day and only get home late at night. My daughter got upset and said I don't want to play with her, and my family also thinks I'm doing some shady business out here...

t_ch: 于是我就想到了拍照这个点子——这样就能证明我确实是在外面工作了！
t_en: So I thought about taking a photo to prove that I'm in fact working during the day!

t_ch: 家家有本难念的经啊。
t_en: Every family has its own troubles.

t_ch: 哈哈哈，其实都是小事！我们一家子关系还是非常好的。
t_en: Hahaha, it's nothing! My family actually gets on very well.

t_ch: 「会社」不该帮人逃避工作吗？
t_en: Isn't the Society all about helping people avoid work?

t_ch: 这么理解可就太片面了！你想啊，「正当权益会社」是帮助人行使「正当权益」的，而我的工作则是确保别人的「正当权益」不受侵犯……
t_en: That's not the whole picture! Think about it — The Rightful Rights Society helps people to exercise their rightful rights, and my job is to ensure people's rights are not violated...

t_ch: 没错，这和「会社」的价值观是不冲突的——「正当权益会社」还是做了它该做的事，没毛病吧？
t_en: There is no conflict between my job and the mission of the Society. The Rightful Rights Society is still doing its thing.

t_ch: 噢哟，不小心扯远了。总之，谢谢你帮忙！这是我的一点心意……
t_en: Anyways, that went off on a tangent. Thank you! These are your rewards...

t_ch: 照片拍完了。
t_en: I took the pictures.

t_ch: 你在告示板的角落里发现了这样一张纸条——
t_en: You found a note in a corner of the notice board that says:

t_ch: 「正当权益会社——会员 查威 发布了一则委托。有意向提供协助的社友请前往面洽！」
t_en: "Chavez, a member of the Rightful Rights Society, has issued an assignment. Please contact Chavez if you are interested in offering your help!"

t_ch: （去找查威问问看吧……）
t_en: (I'll ask Chavez about it...)

t_ch: 我来帮你行使正当权益！
t_en: I'll help you exercise your rightful rights!

t_ch: 啊，原来你也是「正当权益会社」的社员……
t_en: Ah, so you're a member of The Rightful Rights Society too...

t_ch: 我的需求嘛…跟我去趟歌德宾馆就行。我请你喝下午茶，全部消费我来买单——怎么样，很划算吧？
t_en: As for my request... Just come with me to the Goethe Hotel and I'll treat you to afternoon tea — Does that sound like a good deal?

t_ch: 那我可不客气了啊。
t_en: Count me in then.

t_ch: 嘿，要的就是你这种反应。其他社员一听我要请喝茶，都以为我是想趁机推销呢，全都拔腿就跑……
t_en: Aha, that's a reaction I like. The other members all ran away when they heard I wanted to treat them to tea... They thought I'm trying to sell something to them.

t_ch: 你不会是想趁机推销吧？
t_en: Are you trying to sell me something?

t_ch: 停停停——不许聊工作！你们这帮人…怎么都一个反应呀！
t_en: Hold it — Don't talk about work! You... Why do you all react in the same way!?

t_ch: 那为什么要请我喝茶？
t_en: Then why are you inviting me to tea?

t_ch: 哪来那么多为什么——我就是想喝个茶，逃避一下工作，不行吗？「会社」不就是做这个的？
t_en: There is no why — I just want to have some tea and get away from work. Isn't that enough of a reason? Isn't that what the Society does?

t_ch: 毕竟你平时就是做这个的…
t_en: I mean, this is what you do every day...

t_ch: 哎…这我也承认。但我就是想喝个茶，逃避一下工作，不行吗？「会社」不就是做这个的？
t_en: Okay, that's true, but I just want to have some tea and get away from work. Isn't that enough of a reason? Isn't that what the Society does?

t_ch: 行了行了，我的休闲时间是有限的——你先去歌德宾馆大堂等我一下，我收拾收拾就来！
t_en: Alright alright, I only have so much time to relax — Go wait for me in the Goethe Hotel lobby and I'll be there soon!

t_ch: 哟，你可算是到了。没想到我比你还快呢。
t_en: Ahh, you're finally here. Didn't realize I'd be even faster than you.

t_ch: 咳咳…我们先点餐吧。来这种地方歇脚总得点些东西，不然我都不好意思坐在这儿。
t_en: Ahem... Let's order first. You've gotta eat something when you're sitting in places like this, otherwise I'd feel embarrassed.

t_ch: 来，看看菜单——想吃什么想喝什么都随便点，我请客！
t_en: Here's the menu — Order whatever you want. It's my treat!

t_ch: 我要「黑面包汽水冰山」。
t_en: I want a Rye Bread Soda Iceberg.

t_ch: 哦？挺会挑嘛，这是歌德宾馆的爆款呢。黑面包汽水特有的麦香和酸奶的酸甜口感融合在一起…吃一次就会上瘾。
t_en: Oh? You've got good taste. That's the most popular beverage the Goethe Hotel has on offer. A fusion between the unique aroma of the rye bread soda and the sourness of yogurt... One bite is enough to get people hooked.

t_ch: 我要「风味红肠拼盘」。
t_en: I want a Special Sausage Platter.

t_ch: 嗯…这倒是贝洛伯格的特色菜。不过大家都吃腻了，所以没什么人会专门点这种红肠拼盘吃。
t_en: Well... That's indeed a Belobog specialty, but everyone's tired of it. No one really orders that anymore.

t_ch: 我要「歌德经典手冲咖啡」！
t_en: I want a Goethe Classic Hand-Made Coffee.

t_ch: 你怎么回事，这菜单上不写着「暂停供应」嘛。咖啡豆在贝洛伯格可太珍贵了，我也就小时候见妈妈喝过一次…还是帮你点杯「哞哞热牛奶」吧。
t_en: What's up with you? The menu already says it's temporarily unavailable. Coffee beans are so precious in Belobog and I've only ever seen my mother drink coffee once in my life... I'll get you a Moo-Moo Hot Milk instead.

t_ch: 嗯…我的话要这个、这个、还有这个…就这些吧，我去和老歌德说一声。
t_en: Mmm... I want this, this, and this... Alright, I'll go let Old Goethe know.

t_ch: 拉雯妮在老歌德处买了单……
t_en: Lavonne settled the bill with Old Goethe...

t_ch: 老歌德说还得二十分钟，我们先等着吧。
t_en: He says it'll take about <unbreak>20</unbreak> minutes. Let's wait.

t_ch: 你还挺会生活的。
t_en: You sure know how to enjoy life.

t_ch: 哈哈，一般般啦。只是我工作之外的休闲时间不多，平时只能来这歌德宾馆坐坐，自然也就对各种菜式熟悉了。
t_en: Hahaha, not really. I don't have a lot of free time, so when I do have the time, I usually come here and sit. That's how I got familiar with the food on offer.

t_ch: 要开始推销了吗？我准备好了。
t_en: Are you going to sell me something now? I'm ready.

t_ch: 都说了别聊工作！你这人怎么回事啊。
t_en: I said, don't mention work! What's wrong with you?

t_ch: …是不是该聊点什么？
t_en: ...Should we chat about something?

t_ch: 不然呢？我正等你开腔呢。
t_en: Definitely. I was waiting for you to start.

t_ch: 感觉你和平时的状态差别很大。
t_en: You feel quite different from the usual.

t_ch: 嗯…工作是工作，生活是生活嘛。其实……
t_en: Mmm... Well, life is different from work.

t_ch: 我还是不知道该聊什么…
t_en: I don't know what to chat about.

t_ch: …哎，也是。毕竟我们也不熟，我又擅自拉你跑来喝下午茶。这样吧，我就讲讲自己的事好了。
t_en: ...Right, we're not very close friends, and I did drag you here just to have afternoon tea. I'll tell you something about myself then.

t_ch: 拉雯妮开始讲述自己的故事……
t_en: Lavonne starts to talk about herself...

t_ch: 我平时都是独居…独自一人来行政区打拼…当时我就瞅准了城郊那片雪地，觉得一定大有可为……
t_en: I live alone... I came to the Administrative District on my own to make a name for myself... I set my eyes on that snowy area in the suburbs and thought it would be very profitable...

t_ch: 一小时过去……
t_en: One hour later.

t_ch: 你说我一个人打拼是图什么…还不是为了早点经济独立…要再让我回去我可受不了……
t_en: Why do you think I'm trying to make a name for myself... It's all to become financially independent soon... I can't take it if I have to go back...

t_ch: 两小时过去……
t_en: Two hours later.

t_ch: 我觉得自己真的很迷茫…每天拼死拼活推销…舍弃了家庭和生活…可我收获了什么…？
t_en: I feel really lost... I'm doing my best making sales day in, day out... I've given up on having a life or a family... What am I getting out of it?

t_ch: 三小时过去……
t_en: Three hours later.

t_ch: 呼…不吐不快啊。全都说出来舒服多了——多谢你听我说这些有的没的。
t_en: Ahhh... It feels so good to say all that... Thank you for listening to me.

t_ch: 原来你压力这么大…
t_en: I didn't know you were under so much pressure...

t_ch: 哈哈，说出来也就觉得没什么了，谢谢你。多亏了「正当权益会社」啊——能像现在这样忙里偷闲，真是非常宝贵的机会。
t_en: Haha, it feels much better after I've said it. Thank you. The Rightful Rights Society is great — It's a precious chance to have a break like this.

t_ch: 原来你这么能聊…
t_en: I didn't know you were so talkative...

t_ch: 哈哈，我这话匣子一打开就收不住了…不过做房地产中介嘛，这反而是个优点呢。
t_en: Haha, I have problems stopping myself once I start to talk... But that's a good thing when I work as a real estate agent.

t_ch: 哈哈，今天真是挺开心的，谢谢你。多亏了「正当权益会社」啊——能像现在这样忙里偷闲，真是非常宝贵的机会。
t_en: Haha, today has been fun. Thank you. The Rightful Rights Society is great — It's a precious chance to have a break like this.

t_ch: 为了补偿你听我啰啰嗦嗦半天…这是我的一点心意，请你收下吧——我要回去工作了，有空再聊！
t_en: Here's a little something to compensate you for listening to me. I'm heading back to work now. See you later!

t_ch: 你在告示板的角落里发现了这样一张纸条——
t_en: You found a note in a corner of the notice board that says:

t_ch: 「正当权益会社——会员 拉雯妮 发布了一则委托。有意向提供协助的社友请前往面洽！」
t_en: "Lavonne, a member of the Rightful Rights Society, has issued an assignment. Please contact Lavonne if you are interested in offering your help!"

t_ch: （去找拉雯妮问问看吧……）
t_en: (I'll ask Lavonne about it...)

t_ch: 哦？这条街上居然会有人，真稀奇啊。
t_en: Oh? It's rare to see someone walking down this street.

t_ch: 这街上居然会有人，真稀奇啊。
t_en: It's rare to see someone walking down this street.

t_ch: 嚯嚯，想必你也是这商业街的常客了。
t_en: Oho, then you must be a regular to this commercial street, too.

t_ch: 只是，我常在这里散心，却没见过你啊。
t_en: I take my strolls around here, but I've never seen you before.

t_ch: 老人家，裂界里危险啊！
t_en: It's dangerous to be in the Fragmentum!

t_ch: 「裂界」？虽然不知道你在说什么，但我会注意的。谢谢你啊，年轻人。
t_en: Fragmentum? I don't know what you're talking about, but I'll be careful. Thank you, kid.

t_ch: 您就不害怕吗？
t_en: Aren't you scared?

t_ch: 哈哈，这不过是条小小的商业街罢了，哪有什么可害怕的东西呢？
t_en: Haha, it's just a small commercial street. What's there to be scared about?

t_ch: 真是个倔老头。
t_en: What a stubborn old man.

t_ch: 哈哈，既然你如此执着，那我今天便提早些回去吧。
t_en: Haha, I'll head back early if you insist.

t_ch: 不过，你别看我这朽样，我当年也是和阿丽萨大人上过战场的人…没什么能吓倒我，放心吧。
t_en: I may look old and useless now, but I once fought on the battlefield with Madam Alisa... Nothing will faze me.

t_ch: 再加上我已经到了这个岁数…对各种事都不会感到惊奇了。
t_en: Besides, I'm old now. I don't get surprised anymore.

t_ch: 话说回来，我手里有个战前留下的小玩意，能与人隔空通讯，还能凭空留下周遭的画像。只可惜它已经坏了，如今也没人懂得修理的技术……
t_en: Hmm, I do have a gadget with me from before the war. It can help us talk to people across the skies and memorialize your surroundings in images. Pity it's broken now, and no one knows how to fix it anymore...

t_ch: 年轻人，我看你手里那物件与我的很是类似——能拜托你为我这老头子掌掌眼吗？
t_en: Kiddo, what you have in your hand looks similar to my gadget — Can I please ask you to give a good look at this thing?

t_ch: 当然可以。
t_en: Of course.

t_ch: 多谢，年轻人。希望不会占用你太多时间。
t_en: Thank you, kid. I hope this won't take a lot of your time.

t_ch: 嚯嚯嚯…真神奇！想不到我这把老骨头还有看到它亮起来的一天…作为报答，老朽就让你看看我的宝贝吧。
t_en: Ohoho... Amazing! I didn't think my old bones would live to see this light up again... This old one will show you my treasure as a reward.

t_ch: 老人枯槁的手指在液晶屏上颤颤巍巍地四处移动，呼出了似乎是「相册」的应用程序。
t_en: The old man's wrinkled fingers shake as they glide over the display, calling up a program that seems to be an album.

t_ch: 在相册中出现的内容无一例外，全都是一对恩爱的年轻夫妻与惹人怜爱的幼童，只是照片的构图与人物姿势有些微妙的差距。
t_en: All the images in the album show a loving young couple and an adorable child. The only differences between the images are composition and people's pose.

t_ch: 看吧，年轻人。这是我的家人，这位是我的妻子…那时候，真是我人生中最幸福的时候啊。
t_en: Look, kid. This is my family, and this is my wife... That was the happiest period of my life.

t_ch: 可惜妻子她早早离去…我的儿子长大后也嫌我老来不中用，一个人打拼去了。
t_en: But my wife left me early... My son thought I was a bother in my old age and went off to work somewhere else.

t_ch: 您的妻子为何离去了？
t_en: What do you mean, your wife left you?

t_ch: 你…知道寒潮吧？她害了寒病，没能挺过去。
t_en: You know about the Eternal Freeze, right? She got sick from the cold and didn't make it through.

t_ch: 那您还有在和儿子联络吗？
t_en: Are you still in contact with your son?

t_ch: 最开始的那段时间根本联系不上他，但后来我从亲戚那里得到了他的电话，也便有了联络，嚯嚯。
t_en: At first, I couldn't find him at all. Then I got his contact number from a relative and got back in touch, haha.

t_ch: 我的儿子啊…他本想当个法官，哪知道时运不济——最后没了办法，终于挤进了「贝洛伯格供暖」，也算能养活自己了。
t_en: My son had wanted to become a judge, but these are bad times — He had to settle with a position in Belobog's heating system and make a living that way.

t_ch: 在那之后，便陆陆续续能够收到贝洛伯格银行的传信…似乎是那小子为我挣的生活费，嚯嚯嚯。
t_en: I periodically get letters from the Bank of Belobog... Those seem to be money sent to me by my son, hahaha.

t_ch: 他是个好儿子。
t_en: He's a good son.

t_ch: 是啊，是个好儿子……
t_en: Yes, a good son...

t_ch: 他没想过回来与您生活吗？
t_en: Has he ever considered moving back here to live with you?

t_ch: 哎…说实在的，比起钱，我也想过能和儿子一起生活，这或许会更好。但这毕竟要影响他的工作，也就作罢了。
t_en: *sigh* Honestly, instead of receiving money, I did dream about living with him. That might be better for me, but it will affect his job, so I didn't push for it.

t_ch: …就先聊到这里吧。我无端叨扰许久，真是万分抱歉。但也要谢谢你，为我修好了这个小玩意……
t_en: ...Let's stop here. I'm sorry for taking so much of your time. I must thank you for fixing this gadget.

t_ch: 谢谢，年轻人…谢谢。
t_en: Thank you, kid... Thank you.

t_ch: 不客气，你多保重。
t_en: You're welcome. Please take care.

t_ch: 啊！年轻人，请等等……
t_en: Ah! Wait. Kid...

t_ch: 我想多问一句…你每天的这个时间，都会来到商业街上吗？
t_en: I want to ask... Are you going to be here every day at this time?

t_ch: …会的。
t_en: ...Yes, I will.

t_ch: …不会。
t_en: ...No, I won't.

t_ch: 嚯嚯，我知道了……
t_en: Hahaha, I see...

t_ch: 那么，你也多保重。
t_en: Take care of yourself as well.

t_ch: 你转身离开，又正欲回头再看一眼老人时，却发现那人早已杳无踪迹。
t_en: As you turn to leave, you try to get one last look at the old man, but he is already nowhere to be found.

t_ch: 你！就是你！这次你别想再跑掉了！
t_en: You! That's right, you! Don't you run away this time!

t_ch: 你是谁？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 装傻是吧？谁不知道你成天在街上做些偷鸡摸狗的事啊？
t_en: You pretending to be dumb? Everyone knows you've been stealing things left and right!

t_ch: 怎么，想来比划比划？
t_en: What is this? Are you trying to start a fight?

t_ch: 你这小毛贼…口气不小啊！成天偷东西就算了，现在还想暴力拒捕？
t_en: You've got guts, little thief! Not only do you steal all day, you're also trying to resist arrest?

t_ch: 别打了别打了…
t_en: Stop. Just stop...

t_ch: 说什么呢？你只是小偷小摸罢了，不会受皮肉之苦的——除非你暴力拒捕！
t_en: What are you talking about? Theft isn't a serious crime. I won't go tough on you if you don't resist arrest.

t_ch: 我听不懂你在说什么。
t_en: I don't know what you're talking about.

t_ch: 我没偷过东西，你认错人了。
t_en: I didn't steal anything. You've got the wrong person.

t_ch: 行了，别狡辩了，赶紧放弃无谓的抵抗吧！我告诉你，要是你认错态度良好，法务大臣还有网开一面的可能！
t_en: Alright, enough yapping. Just give it up already! I'm telling you, if you admit to your crimes, the Minister of Justice may even let you go!

t_ch: 但如果你执意拒捕…那就别怪我不客气了！
t_en: But if you insist on resisting arrest... then I may have to go tough on you!

t_ch: 怎么会这样…！我、我得赶紧叫兄弟们过来支援……
t_en: How can it be...!? I—I need to call for support...

t_ch: 都说你认错人了。
t_en: I told you, you've got the wrong person.

t_ch: 这怎么可能呢？街上就你一个人，小偷不是你是谁啊……
t_en: No way! You're the only one on the road. Who else would it be...

t_ch: 这裂界里哪来的兄弟？
t_en: Where are you going to get other Guards in the Fragmentum?

t_ch: 什么？裂界？你在说什么……
t_en: What? Fragmentum? What are you talking about...

t_ch: 等等。难道说……
t_en: Wait. Are you saying...

t_ch: 你被困在了过去。
t_en: You're trapped in the past.

t_ch: 我…这怎么可能？什么叫被困在了过去？
t_en: I — How? What do you mean, trapped in the past?

t_ch: 简单来说…你变成幽灵了。
t_en: Put it simply... You are now a ghost.

t_ch: 这里其实是过去的贝洛伯格。
t_en: This is the Belobog of the past.

t_ch: 过去的贝洛伯格？意思是…你是从未来来的？不，不对……
t_en: This is the Belobog of the past? You mean, you're from the future? No, that's not right...

t_ch: 现在的你只是裂界的回声。
t_en: You're now merely an echo in the Fragmentum.

t_ch: 我…可这怎么可能？什么裂界？什么回声？
t_en: I — But how? What Fragmentum? What echo?

t_ch: 难道你的意思是…我已经死了？
t_en: Are you saying that I... I'm dead?

t_ch: 你可以这么理解。
t_en: You can think of it that way, yes.

t_ch: （沉默）
t_en: (Remain Silent)

t_ch: …你说话啊。难道这是真的？
t_en: ...Please say something. Is that true?

t_ch: 这也…太扯淡了吧？
t_en: That's... That can't be real...

t_ch: 那我兄弟们呢？歌德老大呢？阿丽萨大人呢？阿丽萨大人总不可能死了吧？
t_en: What about my brothers-in-arms? Boss Goethe? Madam Alisa? There's no way Madam Alisa would be dead!

t_ch: 她可是…她可是带领我们击败了那些从天上来的怪物们啊！怪物们都没办法伤到她…她怎么会死呢？
t_en: She... She led us to victory against the monsters from the sky! Even the monsters couldn't hurt her... How could she die?

t_ch: 而且，如果大家都死了…我发誓要保护的人们都已经不在了…那我守着这条街又是为了什么？
t_en: And if everyone is dead... If the people I swore to protect are all gone... Then why am I still protecting this street?

t_ch: 自言自语间，这名铁卫渐渐化作齑粉，在空无一人的裂界中散去。
t_en: As the Guard mutters to himself, he gradually turns to dust and dissipates into the empty Fragmentum.

t_ch: 呜…呜呜……
t_en: *sob*... *sniffle*...

t_ch: 爸爸、妈妈…你们去哪了……
t_en: Mom, Dad... Where did you go...

t_ch: 你怎么了？
t_en: What's wrong?

t_ch: 呜呜…我在等爸爸妈妈……
t_en: *sob*... I'm waiting for my Mom and Dad...

t_ch: 这里很危险，快回家吧。
t_en: It's very dangerous here. You should head home.

t_ch: 呜呜…可是…爸爸妈妈还没回来……
t_en: *sob*... But... Mom and Dad aren't back yet...

t_ch: 爸爸妈妈说，他们只会离开一下…马上就回来的……
t_en: Mom and Dad said they'll just be gone for a little bit... They'll be back in a flash...

t_ch: 他们说…让我在这里乖乖等着…可是他们一直都没回来……
t_en: They said... I should be a good kid and wait for them here... But they're still not back...

t_ch: 爸爸妈妈…你们是不是不要我了…？
t_en: Don't Mom and Dad... want me anymore...?

t_ch: 你的爸爸妈妈已经不在了。
t_en: Your Mom and Dad aren't around anymore.

t_ch: 不在…是什么意思…？
t_en: Aren't around... What does that mean?

t_ch: 你已经…不属于这个世界了。
t_en: You... don't belong to this world anymore.

t_ch: 你被困在了…过去。
t_en: You're... stuck in the past.

t_ch: 这是…什么意思…？
t_en: What does that mean...?

t_ch: 我听不懂…我才刚刚上小学…很多事情都不明白……
t_en: I don't get it... I just started grade school. I don't understand a lot of things...

t_ch: 难道爸爸妈妈…真的把我丢掉了吗…？
t_en: Did my Mom and Dad... throw me away?

t_ch: 不要…不要啊…我好害怕…！
t_en: No... No! I'm scared!

t_ch: 在你面前的孩子嚎啕大哭了起来。当你正准备俯身、想要摸头安慰她时，却见自己的指尖穿透了她幼小的身躯。
t_en: The child before you starts to wail. As you bend down and try to pat her head in comfort, you see your fingers go through her small figure.

t_ch: 随后，这被困于裂界的小小回声很快便化作微光、四散无踪了。
t_en: Then, this tiny echo trapped in the Fragmentum scatters into particles of light and disappears.

t_ch: 我见过他们了，他们说…
t_en: I saw them. They said...

t_ch: 你见过我的爸爸妈妈？他们说什么了…？
t_en: You saw my Mom and Dad? What did they say?

t_ch: 他们马上回来，你要乖乖等着。
t_en: They'll be back soon. Just wait right here.

t_ch: 呜、呜呜…我明白了……
t_en: *sob* O—Okay...

t_ch: 我会…会在这里一直等着…哪里也不会去……
t_en: I'll... I'll stay here. I won't go anywhere...

t_ch: 在你面前的孩子嚎啕大哭了起来。当你正准备俯身、想要摸头安慰她时，却见自己的指尖穿透了她幼小的身躯。
t_en: The child before you starts to wail. As you bend down and try to pat her head in comfort, you see your fingers go through her small figure.

t_ch: 随后，这被困于裂界的小小回声很快便化作微光、四散无踪了。
t_en: Then, this tiny echo trapped in the Fragmentum scatters into particles of light and disappears.

t_ch: 他们正在商业街入口等你。
t_en: They're waiting for you at the entrance of the shopping street.

t_ch: 呜、呜呜…我明白了……
t_en: *sob* O—Okay...

t_ch: 我…我会马上去找爸爸妈妈…！谢谢你…！
t_en: I—I'll go find Mom and Dad! Thank you!

t_ch: 在你面前的孩子抹了把泪，向你指示的方向奔去——仿佛她的父母确实仍在等候——仿佛这裂界描摹生成的旧日街道依旧真实存在。
t_en: The child before you wipes her tears away and runs toward the direction you pointed to as if her parents are indeed waiting for her, as if these foregone streets sculpted by the Fragmentum indeed still exist.

t_ch: 你望见这被困于裂界的小小回声遁入街巷深处，随后很快便化作微光、四散无踪了。
t_en: You watch as this tiny echo trapped in the Fragmentum dives between the alleyways, then fades into scattered particles of light.

t_ch: 老朽手里也有个类似的小玩意，是战前留下的——它能使人隔空通讯，还能凭空留下周遭的画像。只可惜它已经坏了，如今也没人懂得修理的技术……
t_en: I have a similar gadget with me from before the war. It can help us talk to people across the skies and memorialize your surroundings in images. Pity it's broken now, and no one knows how to fix it anymore...

t_ch: 年轻人，我看你手里那物件与我的很是类似——能拜托你为我这老头子掌掌眼吗？
t_en: Kiddo, what you have in your hand looks similar to my gadget — Can I please ask you to give a good look at this thing?

t_ch: 当然可以。
t_en: Of course.
